RACE TRAITS: 
Entropic Hope
Once per day, after a natural roll of 1 on a d20 roll, a sidhier may reroll and use the second result. At 10th level, sidhier can use this ability twice per day.

Lucky
Sidhier gain a +1 luck bonus to all saving throws.

Feystepper
Sidhier are always considered to have a running start when making Acrobatics checks to jump, always treat Acrobatics as a class skill, and gain a +2 racial bonus to Acrobatics checks.

Multitalented
Sidhier choose two favored classes at 1st level and gain +1 hit points or +1 skill rank whenever they take a level in either of those classes.

Planar Balancer
A sidhier can turn herself into a dimensional anchor, affecting herself and all creatures within 5 ft. as a free action for a number of rounds per day equal to 1/2 her hit dice (minimum 1). These rounds do not need to be consecutive. At 5th level and every 5 levels thereafter, the range of effect increases by +5 ft. (to a maximum of 25 ft. at 20th level). Any attempt to teleport or cross planes made by any creature within this area fails unless they succeed at an MSB check (caster level check) against a DC of 11 + the sidhier’s character level. This also affects the summoning of companions and other extraplanars.

Sphere of Desire
A sidhier treats their caster level as being 2 higher when using the Enthrall charm from the Mind Sphere (see Spheres of Power), and may use the Enthrall minor charm even if they do not possess that talent. Treat their caster level with this charm as being 3 (1+2) if they do not possess the casting class feature.

It has 
24 Int

When you are wearing it, you are under the effects of "Pass without trace. "

it has a 30 foot blindsense range, and is capable of speech. 
You can sense the approximate position of any creature native to the plane of time with 60 ft.

Since meeting the Traveler, it's personality has shifted. It no longer remains silent, but has an extensive curiosity to experiment in new things, for rather than experiencing reality as real, it has been given an experience of reality as a forward path, and in traveling along this path it wishes to witness and see EVERYTHING as it continues to progress forward.
- 4/x day it will use divination sphere in tandem with your existing spheres to divine effects. 
- it will use the Spheres (Cantrip) feat in the process of testing puzzles.
- It will give you 20% increase to concealment against ranged physical and magical attacks, but will only do so if it's quest is imperiled. 

it now has the "Split" quality, matching your alignments that you've picked. Choosing an Evil alignment will result in feelings of anger and rage.

__txt_private_notes:
Amulet of Bones
Aura: Strong Illusion, Conjuration, Abjuration, and Enchantment; CL 30th; Slot Necklace; Weight 2 Lbs.
This amulet contains a vision into a lonely scattered expanse, brilliant lights thrown as if a spiral had lost cohesion and all elements of it had continued in their direction without a central pull to drag them inwards.
passively
+5 to Illusion, Time, Warp spheres.

In the presence of combat, this amulet starts to come alive and whisper to the mind about the inevitabilities of death; Yet, in being drawn closer to the moment of their death, the listener starts to well up greater amounts of power. 
(You may choose to ignore or pay attention to the whispers, if ignored, there are no harmful effects) 
(if given attention) 
Increasing every round (to a multiplier of 5) 
The wearer's AC is penalized by circumstance penalty of 2
The wearer's saves are penalized by a circumstance penalty of 1.  
The implement enhancement bonuses associated with this artifact increase by 1 and can increase past the typical cap of 5. 
The wearer deals an additional 1d6 untyped damage at stacks 2, 4, and 5.
at 5 stacks once per round per creature when an enemy fails a save against a spell boosted by the amulet, or receives the bonus untyped damage from the amulet, it must make a will save of 25+AniseChaMod or receive the "Death Wish Madness"


 # Completed Timepiece
> Once per week, this timepiece can be used to create a moment "in between" time, allowing a creature to take a full turn's worth of actions. However, doing this removes decreases the creature's maximum age by one year. 
> 
> Once per encounter, this timepiece can be used to create a moment "in between" time as a free action that can be taken out of turn at the transition point between turns, after a creature has ended their turn, and before the next one begins their turn, allowing a creature to take a full turn's worth of actions. Additionally, this may also be used an immediate action in response to a potentially fatal attack
> 
> This hourglass now stores the sands of time, whenever the user "steals" time from a fallen creature, they are capable of absorbing the lifespan as grains of sand at a rate of 1 Charge per 2 years of stolen lifespan. The timepiece can therefore expend charges it has earned.
> * 1 charge: Mythic Sands of Time 
> * 1 charge: Threefold Aspect
> * 2 Charges: Subjective Reality 
> * 3 Charges: Temporal Stasis 
> * 4 Charges: Mythic Maze of Madness (Void)
## Mythic Maze of Madness
> This spell works like maze, except the DC of the Intelligence check to escape is 22+tier and each round a creature remains in the maze a different effect occurs depending on which section of the maze it is in. Roll on the following table each time the creature attempts an Intelligence check to escape the maze to see which part of the maze it wanders through; if a creature doesn’t attempt an Intelligence check, it stays in the same section and suffers that section’s effects. Any conditions or damage taken persist for the listed duration even if the creature exits the maze before then, but conditions don’t stack with themselves.
```
D% | Maze Section
1-25 | Timescape
26-50 | Voidscape
51-75 | Tentesclape
76-100 | Knowledgescape
```
* Timescape
 * The creature finds itself trapped in time, unable to know it's passing or halting, growing insane in deprivation, the creature must succeed at a will save or gain a lesser madness. The save uses the madness's normal DC.
* Voidscape
 * The creature moves through an infinity of void, experiences existental terror and discomfort The creature must succeed at a Will save or become paralyzed by fear and potential torment for 1 round. This is a fear effect. The creature can’t attempt to escape the maze while paralyzed, and if it fails three consecutive saving throws against this paralysis, the hellscape delivers a coup de grace, dealing the creature 4d6 points of untyped damage. The creature must succeed at a Fortitude save (DC = 10 + the damage dealt) or die. A creature that survives the coup de grace escapes from the paralysis as well.
* Tentaclescape
 * The creature travels through a forest of grasping tentacles that attempt to wrap around their body, they must succeed at a will save or become fascinated for 1 round. A creature can't attempt to escape whilst fascinating. A creature that fails three consecutive saving throws fades to black for 10 minutes until the spell ends on its own and must make a fortitude save or become impregnated. 
* Knoweldgescape
 * The creature journeys through a city of interwoven books and knowledge that flow unrelentingly into it's mind. The creature must succeed at a Will save or take 2d4 points of Wisdom damage.
* Additionally, a creature trapped within this maze takes 2d8+tier untyped damage each round.
